label: yes
cot_prefix: Okay, let's see. Jane was born on the last day of February in 2001. Today is her 16th birthday. So first, I need to figure out her birthday date and then determine what the date was a month ago from today. Wait, the last day of February is the 28th in a non-leap year and the 29th in a leap year. But 2001 is not a leap year because leap years are every 4 years starting from 2000. So 2001 isn't. Therefore, her birthday is February 28, 2001. Now, today is her 16th birthday. That would mean today's date is February 28, 2017 (since 2001 + 16 = 2017). But wait, wait. Let me check that again. If she was born in 2001, then adding 16 years would be 2001 + 16 = 2017. So her 16th birthday is in 2017. But February 28, 2017. Is that correct? But the next step is to figure out the date a month ago from today. So if today is February 28, 2017, then subtracting one month would take us to January 28, 2017. Because a month ago from February 28 would be January 28. Let me think: if today is the 28th of February, then a month ago is the 28th of January. So that would be 01/28/2017. Looking at the options, option D is 01/28/2017. So that's option D. But wait, let me double-check. Let's make sure the current date is correct. Jane was born on 2/28/2001. Her 16th birthday would be 2/28/2017. So a month before that is 1/28/2017. That seems right. Because if you go back one month from February 28, you land on January 28. January has 31 days, so no issues there. So the correct answer should be D. Let me check the options again. Option D is 01/28/2017. Yeah. The other options don't match.
